metaTitle: The Boeing Atmosphere Global Documentation Hub - Components - Menu

header.title: Menu
header.description: Menus display a list of choices on a temporary surface

items: Use menus to show items in a scannable way
Make menus easy to open, close, and select from
Menus can open from a variety of components, including icon buttons and text fields

data.resource: Figma: Atmosphere Foundations and Components Library

sections.textBefore:
Configurations

Menus appear when users interact with a button, action, or other control.

sections.text:
Usage

Use a menu to display a list of choices on a temporary surface, such as a set of overflow actions in a top app bar. A menu opens when users interact with an element, such as an icon, button, or an input field, or when they perform a specific action.

Menus display a list of choices on a temporary surface.

sections.textAfter: Menus let users make a selection from multiple options. They’re less prominent and take up less space than a set of radio buttons or choice chips. Use the leading icon to communicate whether the list element is active or inactive.

1. List element is inactive - on hover
  2. List element is inactive - pressed
  3. Upon opening the menu again, list element is now active

sections.imgAlt: Menus appear in front of all other permanent UI elements.
sections.textAfter: Menus appear in front of all other permanent UI elements.

sections.text:
Position

Position menus relative to the edge of the screen or browser. In general, menus appear below, next to, or in front of the element that opens them.

If the menu position means it might be cut off by the browser or the edge of the screen, it can appear to the left, right, or above the element that opens it instead.

sections.textAfter:
1. List item
  2. List item leading icon
  3. List item trailing iconList item trailing text
  4. Container
  5. Divider
  6. Profile header
  7. Supporting text

sections.text:
Container & list items

Menu containers hold all menu items.

Menus display lists of options. List items can include label text, leading icons, trailing icons or trailing text.

List items have interaction states including hover, focus, and press.

Avoid hiding list items. If a list item is only available in certain cases, display it as disabled. This way, users know that the option is available in certain cases.

Dividers

You can use dividers to group list items.

Displaying menus

A menu display when a user taps either an interactive element (such as an icon, button, or action) or content (such as selected items or text).

A menu’s position on screen affects where and how it will appear. If opened at the top of the screen, it will expand downwards (to avoid being cropped).

Filtering

A menu with filtering is also known as autocomplete or a combo box. As the user begins typing, menu options filter based on what's entered. This can help users quickly find the right option from a large list.

Scrolling

If not all menu items are displayed at once, menus can be scrollable. In this state, menus show a persistent scrollbar.

Selection

When a menu is opened from a button or icon button, the button or icon button should visually remain the same.

images.text: When a menu is opened from a text input field, the selected menu item can be displayed in that field. In some cases, the field can accept and display user input (whether or not it’s listed as a menu choice).

sections.text:
Use cases

Users should be able to:

  • Clearly understand each of the menu items and their function
  • Navigate to, open, and close a menu with assistive technology
  • Navigate between and select menu items with assistive technology

Keyboard navigation

Users should be able to navigate and activate the elements inside the Menu using these keyboard keys:

data.actions: For closed menus: Opens menu or submenu / For open menus: Selects list item
To select a focused radio option not yet selected
Focus moves to the next list item starting with letter

sections.text:
Using labels

Accessibility labels are used with assistive technology devices like screen readers. The accessibility label should align with each list item’s text label.

sections.textBefore:
Focus

Initial focus lands on the element that holds the menu.
sections.textAfter:
1. First tab lands on menu
  2. Space or Enter opens the menu or submenu
  3. p and down arrow moves focus on the menu items
  4. Space or Enter closes the menu or submenu

1. Disabled menu items cannot receive focus
  2. A divider cannot receive focus

sections.text:
Densities

The menu can be used in two densities, relaxed and condensed. All of these changes can be visually seen in the Measurements section.
